title: 10 best herbal creams for grey hair

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying and hair loss.
Stimulates blood flow and circulation: Rosemary's essential oils improve blood circulation to the scalp, which may help to nourish and promote healthy hair growth, potentially reducing grey hair.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is often associated with grey hair and may contribute to its progression.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with grey hair because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Rosemary essential oil stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing graying. The herb's ability to boost circulation and nourish hair follicles may also contribute to thicker, more vibrant hair. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help protect hair from environmental stressors and damage, potentially slowing down the onset of grey hair. This natural approach may help maintain a full, youthful head of hair.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: Carnosic acid, a phenolic diterpene, has been shown to stimulate hair growth and reduce graying by inhibiting the activity of 5-lipoxygenase, an enzyme involved in the production of pro-inflammatory compounds that can contribute to hair graying.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a polyphenolic compound, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, promoting a healthier environment for hair growth and potentially slowing down or reversing graying.

Camphor: Camphor, a terpene, has been traditionally used to stimulate blood flow and promote hair growth by increasing the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the scalp, which can help to nourish and revitalize grey hair follicles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rosmarinus officinalis leaves are used to make creams for grey hair due to their high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to promote hair growth and reduce graying.
Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are used in creams for grey hair as they contain rosmarinic acid, a compound that helps to reduce oxidative stress and promote hair pigmentation.

Stems: Rosmarinus officinalis stems are used in creams for grey hair because they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to protect hair follicles from damage and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from a trusted source.
Infuse 250ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil with 50g of rosmarinus officinalis leaves for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 50g of shea butter with 25g of beeswax and 25g of cocoa butter in a double boiler.
Combine the infused oil with the melted shea butter mixture and allow it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal tea creams containing antioxidants, such as those found in green tea or ginkgo biloba, help neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and contribute to grey hair.
Vasodilators: Herbs like rosemary and thyme have vasodilator properties, which help increase blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of grey hair.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal tea creams containing anti-inflammatory compounds, such as those found in chamomile or calendula, help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation that can contribute to premature greying.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with grey hair because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb has been traditionally used to promote hair growth and reduce graying. The high levels of catechins and polyphenols in Camellia sinensis help to nourish and protect the hair follicles, promoting a healthy scalp and hair growth. Regular application of Camellia sinensis creams may help to slow down the graying process and maintain a natural hair color, giving the appearance of a fuller and more vibrant head of hair.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, specifically epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can slow down or prevent grey hair formation.
Theaflavins: Theaflavins, formed during the fermentation process, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect hair follicles and promote melanocyte health.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Camellia sinensis, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and promote hair growth, potentially slowing down or reversing grey hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antioxidant and astringent properties, which help to tone and condition the hair, reducing the appearance of grey hair.
Flowers: Employed for their ability to stimulate hair growth and improve scalp health, leading to a reduction in grey hair.
Stems: Utilized for their high content of flavonoids, which help to protect the hair follicles and promote healthy hair growth, reducing the visibility of grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Steep 8 ounces of camellia sinensis leaves in 1 quart of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve into a heat-resistant container.
Combine 1/4 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of glycerin in a small bowl.
Mix the camellia sinensis liquid with the glycerin mixture and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 5 to 7 minutes until it thickens and emulsifies.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and irritation on the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and helping to address the underlying causes of grey hair.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in aloe vera neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and contribute to premature graying, allowing for healthier, more resilient hair.

Stimulatory properties: Aloe vera's stimulatory compounds promote blood flow to the scalp, stimulating hair growth and helping to reverse grey hair by increasing melanin production and reducing the appearance of white hairs.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with grey hair because of its rich nutrient content and ability to nourish the scalp. The vitamins, minerals, and amino acids present in aloe vera help to promote healthy hair growth and improve the overall texture of the hair. By strengthening the hair follicles and promoting blood circulation, aloe vera creams can help to reduce the appearance of grey hair and give the hair a more vibrant and youthful appearance.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds may help to promote hair growth by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to grey hair.

Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect the hair follicles from damage and promote healthy hair growth, reducing the appearance of grey hair.

Vitamins and minerals (Biotin, Vitamin C, and Vitamin E): These nutrients are essential for maintaining healthy hair growth and may help to reduce grey hair by promoting the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part to make creams for grey hair, as they contain a high concentration of aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to condition and stimulate hair growth.

Gel from the Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe vera is also widely used to make creams for grey hair, as it has moisturizing properties that help to nourish and hydrate the scalp and hair.

Extract from the Leaves: The extract from the leaves of Aloe vera, rich in vitamins and minerals, is used in creams for grey hair to promote healthy hair growth and to reduce the appearance of grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of pure aloe vera gel from 2 to 3 mature plants and 20g of beeswax.
Combine the aloe vera gel with 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until the oil dissolves.
Add 10g of shea butter and 5g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well.
Gradually add 20g of beeswax to the mixture and continue heating until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken before transferring it to a container.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal maidenhair tree creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage hair follicles and lead to premature graying.
Vasodilators: The creams contain vasodilators that improve blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing gray hair due to poor circulation.
Stimulants: Herbal maidenhair tree creams have stimulant properties that can help increase hair growth by stimulating the hair follicles and promoting a healthier, thicker coat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with grey hair because it promotes blood circulation to the scalp, stimulating hair follicles and encouraging healthy hair growth. remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: A triterpene saponin found in Ginkgo biloba, bilobalide may help reduce oxidative stress and promote hair growth, potentially slowing down or reversing grey hair.

Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound, quercetin has antioxidant properties that can help protect hair follicles from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy scalp environment, which may contribute to maintaining natural hair color.
Ginkgolides: A group of sesquiterpene lactones, ginkgolides have been shown to improve blood flow to the scalp, potentially promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of grey hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve circulation and promote hair growth.
Seeds: Utilized for their antioxidant properties, which may help to protect hair follicles and promote healthy hair growth.
Barks: Employed for their potential to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which may contribute to a reduction in grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Combine 250g of shea butter, 100g of coconut oil, and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts completely.
Add 50g of ginkgo biloba extract to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool to around 40°c for 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool to room temperature before applying.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cornflower extract has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate skin irritations, which could potentially benefit the scalp and help soothe any irritation associated with grey hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Centaurea cyanus, also known as cornflower, creams helps with grey hair because of its rich antioxidant properties and ability to promote healthy scalp and hair follicles. The herb's flavonoids and phenolic acids help to nourish and protect the hair, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to premature greying. By promoting a healthy scalp environment, Centaurea cyanus creams can help to slow down or even reverse the greying process, leaving hair looking healthier, shinier, and more vibrant.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, found in Centaurea cyanus, may help with grey hair by promoting melanogenesis, the process of melanin production in hair follicles.

Phenolic acids (caffeic acid, ferulic acid): Phenolic acids in Centaurea cyanus may help with grey hair by acting as antioxidants, protecting hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to premature graying.

remedy_parts: Flowers: Used in skincare products due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Seeds: Used in herbal remedies and skincare products for their potential antioxidant and moisturizing properties.
Leaves: Used in herbal remedies and skincare products due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowers of centaurea cyanus in the morning after the dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried flowers in 1 cup of carrier oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler until melted.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of melissa leaves from mature plants in the early morning.
Dry the melissa leaves in a warm place for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content.
Infuse 1/4 cup of dried melissa leaves in 2 cups of distilled water for 15 minutes.
Strain the melissa infusion through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to create a liquid extract.
Blend 1/4 cup of the melissa liquid extract with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with grey hair because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_constituents: Stigmasterol: This phytosterol helps to reduce the conversion of melanin to eumelanin, thereby promoting the growth of new hair follicles and potentially slowing down the graying process.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to promote hair growth and reduce grey hair.

remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried urtica dioica leaves and flowers and grind them into a fine powder in a coffee grinder.
Combine the ground powder with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and slowly add 10g of distilled water while stirring the mixture with a spatula.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in full sun and dry them thoroughly for 2-3 weeks to preserve their medicinal properties.
Combine 1 cup of dried calendula flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar occasionally to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid oil.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: This helps to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy hair growth.

Barks: They contain phenolic compounds that may help stimulate hair growth and improve scalp health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 pound of dried roots of echinacea angustifolia in the fall when the plants are mature.
Dry the roots further at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours to remove any remaining moisture.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 10 minutes to make the infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth and combine it with 8 ounces of aloe vera gel in a mixing bowl.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes until it becomes a smooth and consistent cream.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of taraxacum officinale flowers at peak potency for best results within 2 weeks.
Steep the flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the mixture and combine 1 cup of the liquid with 1/2 cup of distilled water.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil to the liquid mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with grey hair because it contains silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that nourishes and protects the hair follicles. As we age, our hair's pigmentation can fade, leading to graying. Silymarin in milk thistle cream stimulates hair growth, promotes melanin production, and strengthens hair strands, effectively reversing grey hair. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh silybum marianum leaves and flowers, ensuring sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Combine 20 grams of dried silybum marianum aerial parts with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least four hours or overnight, shaking the jar occasionally to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining 50 milliliters of the herbal extract.

Blend the herbal extract with 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of shea butter in a double boiler, heating until smooth and creamy, allowing to cool before transferring to airtight containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with grey hair because it contains hyperforin, a unique compound that has been shown to stimulate melanocyte activity, increasing pigment production and promoting natural hair color. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry hypericum perforatum leaves to ensure optimal potency for future use.
Grind 20 grams of dried hypericum perforatum leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered hypericum perforatum with 10 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aesculus hippocastanum leaves in the morning when dew is still present for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a warm air circulation method at 40 degrees celsius for two hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried aesculus hippocastanum leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the powdered aesculus hippocastanum with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil and 10 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of cissus quadrangularis leaves, wash them thoroughly with distilled water and pat dry with paper towels.
Combine the dried cissus quadrangularis leaves with 200g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass container and simmer for 4 hours over low heat.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another heat-resistant glass container, pressing on the solids to extract as much oil as possible.
Add 50ml of sweet almond oil and 10ml of beeswax pellets to the extracted oil, melt them in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly and let cool at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with grey hair because they stimulate blood flow to the scalp, nourishing and revitalizing the hair follicles. The herbal extracts in these creams, such as elderberry, rosemary, and lavender, help to promote melanin production, which is responsible for hair pigmentation. As a result, the growth of new hair can help to cover grey strands, leaving hair looking healthier, shinier, and more vibrant. Regular use of elder creams can also improve hair thickness and texture, reducing the appearance of grey hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elder flowers contain powerful antioxidants that help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying and loss of pigmentation.

Vasoconstrictive properties: The compounds found in elder flowers, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have a vasoconstrictive effect on the blood vessels near the scalp, which helps improve circulation and promote healthy hair growth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder creams contain anti-inflammatory agents that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to grey hair and promote healthy hair regrowth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, elder roots help to nourish and protect the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of grey hair.
Barks: The bark of the elder plant contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to tighten and strengthen hair follicles, reducing the appearance of grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves, ensuring proper identification and contamination control measures.
Dry the harvested botanicals using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Combine one part dried sambucus nigra with two parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes, then strain through cheesecloth to remove solids and filter the liquid.
Transfer the filtered oil extract to glass jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks before using as an herbal cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with grey hair because they stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy follicles and pigmentation. The menthol in peppermint also cools and soothes the scalp, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to premature greying. Additionally, peppermint's natural antioxidants help protect the hair from environmental damage, preserving its natural color and vibrancy. By using herbal peppermint creams, individuals can potentially slow down or even reverse grey hair, leaving them with a more youthful and radiant appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Peppermint contains antioxidants like vitamin C and E, which help neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and cause premature graying.

Stress-relieving properties: The menthol in peppermint has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing stress hormones that can contribute to grey hair by accelerating melanin depletion from hair follicles.

Inflammation-reducing properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help soothe scalp irritations and reduce inflammation, which can cause hair loss and graying.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are valued for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in promoting healthy scalp circulation and reducing grey hair.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain flavonoids that help improve blood circulation to the scalp, which can stimulate melanin production and reduce grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh leaves of peppermint and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Blend the peppermint leaves with 100ml of distilled water in a blender for 30 seconds at high speed.
Steep the blended mixture in a saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes to release essential oils.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and add 50g of coconut oil for 2 hours.
Whip the mixture until creamy, adding 20 drops of lavender essential oil for a soothing aroma.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with grey hair because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which promote healthy scalp circulation and follicle stimulation. The plant's allantoin content also nurtures the hair roots, encouraging a natural color restoration. As comfrey cream is absorbed into the skin, it provides a protective barrier that soothes and calms the scalp, creating an ideal environment for melanin production to occur. This can lead to a reduction in grey hair appearance and even promote new hair growth with its natural pigmentation.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey cream contains allantoin, a compound that stimulates collagen production and promotes skin elasticity, helping to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles associated with grey hair.
Tannins: Tannins in comfrey cream have been shown to improve blood circulation and nourish the scalp, which can help stimulate melanin production and promote healthy, natural hair color.

Inulin: Inulin, a prebiotic fiber found in comfrey, helps support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which is linked to overall health and wellbeing, including hair health and potentially preventing premature graying.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a natural humectant that helps retain moisture and promote healthy skin cells.
Leaves: High in rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that combats free radicals and promotes hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality symphytum officinale leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them thinly on paper towels and storing them in a dry place for 7 days.
Grind 20g of dried symphytum officinale leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 30ml of sweet almond oil and heat the mixture to 180°f in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Add 1g of ground symphytum officinale leaves to the heated wax-oil mixture and stir well before pouring into sterilized containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with grey hair because they contain the powerful antioxidant, ferulic acid, which is known to promote melanin production in the hair follicles. Vitamins and minerals: Beach rose creams often contain vitamins B and E, as well as minerals like copper and zinc, which are essential for healthy hair growth and maintenance, helping to promote natural pigmentation and reduce the appearance of grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, roots help to nourish and strengthen hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing premature greying.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa petals from a pesticide-free garden for optimal potency.
Combine one part dried rosa rugosa flowers with two parts coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 20-30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth to obtain the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 1:0.5:0.25, heating until smooth and melted.
Pour the cream into glass jars and refrigerate for at least four weeks before use to allow it to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with grey hair because of its natural ability to promote healthy hair growth and nourishment. The herb's high concentration of antioxidants and vitamins A, C, and E help to strengthen hair follicles, reducing the appearance of grays and promoting a thicker, shinier coat. Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can cause grey hairs and slow down melanin production.

Stimulates melanocyte activity: Fennel's flavonoids and other bioactive compounds have been shown to stimulate melanocyte activity, which can help promote hair pigmentation and reduce the appearance of grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oxidative stress on grey hair.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel leaves from your garden or purchase dried fennel powder from a health food store.
Measure out two tablespoons of dried fennel powder and combine it with one cup of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler setup over medium heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally until the mixture thickens into a rich paste.
Strain the cooled paste through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove any excess liquid and transfer it to a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Mix in one teaspoon of coconut oil and one teaspoon of shea butter into the fennel paste, then seal the jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with grey hair because they contain antioxidants and essential oils that nourish and stimulate the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to premature graying. Leaves: High in vitamin E and fatty acids, the leaves of the sweet marjoram plant moisturize and condition the scalp, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum majorana leaves and stems and dry them in a cool dark place for 7 days.
Combine 20g of dried origanum majorana with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5g of shea butter to the infused oil and melt over low heat for 30 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into sterilized jars and let it set at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for grey hair are natural topical treatments that help to slow down or reverse the greying process by nourishing and protecting the hair follicles. The best herbal creams for grey hair include Curcuma longa, Aloe barbadensis, Cassia auriculata, Santalum album, Rosmarinus officinalis, Avena sativa, Urtica dioica, Ginkgo biloba, Aloe vera, and Emblica officinalis. If left untreated, grey hair can lead to feelings of insecurity, low self-esteem, and social isolation, negatively impacting individuals' lives and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to grey hair that are treated with herbal creams are various skin conditions such as psoriasis, eczema, and rosacea. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. These creams can also be used to treat minor skin injuries, acne, and burns, providing natural relief and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with grey hair is a blend of Amla oil, Bhringaraj oil, and Triphala oil. Amla oil rich in vitamin C promotes hair growth and prevents graying. Bhringaraj oil stimulates hair follicles and reduces stress, a common cause of premature graying. Triphala oil balances the scalp's pH, preventing damage and promoting healthy hair growth. Apply a mixture of equal parts Amla and Bhringaraj oil followed by a few drops of Triphala oil to massage into the scalp for optimal results.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Plants (Basel, Switzerland), the use of herbal creams, specifically rice bran oil from the Thai rice variety BB4CMU, has shown to be beneficial for grey hair. The study involved analyzing the in vitro hair revitalization properties of rice bran oil, defatted rice bran extract, and rice husk from local rice cultivars in northern Thailand. The results found that BB4CMU-RBO exhibited a comparable induction of melanin production on melanocytes (130.18 ± 9.13% of control) and a significant inhibitory effect on both steroid 5α-reductase genes, suggesting its potential in promoting hair growth and re-pigmentation effects.
